title: STDIO, Streamable HTTP, Lifecycle, and Deployment · Protocol Repair Bay
summary: Choose and configure a transport for local and remote MCP servers. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a local and remote transport deployment plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement newline-safe STDIO transport without logging protocol noise to standard output.
Implement remote Streamable HTTP with correct routing, content types, and transport security.
Handle startup, initialization, shutdown, timeout, and reconnect behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-observability-170r4i: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Observability into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-observability-170r4i:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Observability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Log identifiers, latency, errors, and side effects without leaking protocol secrets.
